message:
fix(store,server,runtime): negative-zero score canonicalization, non-finite blocking timeout rejection, and Lua error message fidelity

Sorted set negative-zero fix:
- Add canonicalize_zero_score() to ScoreMember::cmp so -0.0 and +0.0
  sort identically, preventing members stored with -0 from becoming
  invisible to ZRANGEBYSCORE queries that use 0 as a bound
- ZSCORE now returns "0" instead of "-0" for members stored with -0
- Add tests for both the ZSCORE display and ZRANGEBYSCORE bound behavior

Blocking command hardening:
- Extract parse_blocking_deadline() helper shared by BLPOP, BLMOVE,
  BLMPOP, and WAIT/WAITAOF timeout parsing
- Reject non-finite timeout values (NaN, inf, -inf) that previously
  parsed as valid f64 and could produce nonsensical deadlines
- Add tests for parse_blocking_deadline edge cases and a full
  try_build_blocked_state rejection test with BLPOP + inf timeout

Lua scripting improvement:
- redis.pcall error tables now contain the human-readable RESP error
  string (e.g. "WRONGTYPE ...") instead of the Rust Debug format,
  matching Redis behavior for Lua error introspection

Runtime cleanup:
- Remove unnecessary parentheses around CommandError variants in .to_resp()
  calls across the entire runtime crate (cosmetic, no behavior change)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
